name: writing-bots
description: Write a bot to continuously listen and respond to events on a public blockchain network.
capabilities: Guide users through blockchain bot design patterns
Explain event handling for blocks, logs, and cron triggers
Advise on risk management and circuit breakers
Describe state management for bot operations
author: ApeWorX
securitySummary: This skill is a pure markdown prompt file providing guidance for designing blockchain bots using the Silverback SDK. All static findings are false positives. The scanner misidentified standard blockchain terminology (keccak256, sha256, mainnet) as cryptographic weaknesses, CLI documentation as command injection, and documentation URLs as network exfiltration. The skill contains no executable code and advises best practices including using environment variables and circuit breakers for safety.
valueStatement: Creating blockchain bots requires understanding event-driven architecture and risk management. This skill guides AI assistants to help users design, implement, and deploy automated bots that respond to on-chain events.
userTitle: Create blockchain monitoring bots with Silverback
limitations: Does not execute blockchain transactions directly
Requires user to install Silverback CLI separately
Does not provide pre-built bot templates
Does not access blockchain networks directly

useCases.title: Monitor DeFi protocols
useCases.description: Build bots that track pool metrics and alert on unusual activity

useCases.title: Automate trading responses
useCases.description: Create bots that execute trades based on on-chain signals

useCases.title: Bridge and sync systems
useCases.description: Design bots that sync blockchain state with external services

promptTemplates.title: Simple event monitor
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a Silverback bot that listens for new blocks on Ethereum mainnet and logs block numbers and timestamps.

promptTemplates.title: Event filter bot
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs smart contract event monitoring
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a bot that watches for specific smart contract events on Uniswap and sends Discord notifications when trades occur.

promptTemplates.title: Risk-managed automation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a bot with circuit breaker protection and configurable limits via environment variables for automated trading.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-chain aggregator
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a bot that monitors multiple chains, tracks derived metrics, and triggers actions based on complex threshold conditions.

bestPractices: Start with print debugging before adding automated actions
Use environment variables for all configurable limits and keys
Implement circuit breakers for emergency stop conditions
Test thoroughly before configuring production signer keys
antiPatterns: Skipping documentation checks for current SDK version
Running bots with production keys before testing
Ignoring error logs during initial deployment
Hardcoding private keys or secrets in bot code

faq.question: Which blockchain networks does Silverback support?
faq.answer: Silverback supports any EVM-compatible network including Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, and Optimism.

faq.question: What programming language is used for Silverback bots?
faq.answer: Silverback bots are written in Python using the Ape framework for blockchain interaction.

faq.question: How do I connect external services like Discord or Telegram?
faq.answer: Use Python client libraries in your event handlers to send messages via webhooks or bot APIs.

faq.question: Is it safe to run bots with signing capabilities?
faq.answer: Only after thorough testing. Use environment variables for keys and implement circuit breakers for safety.

faq.question: Why is my bot not receiving events?
faq.answer: Check network configuration, verify contract addresses, and review logs for connection or filter errors.

faq.question: How does this compare to other blockchain automation tools?
faq.answer: Silverback offers full Python flexibility for custom logic whereas alternatives may have limited scripting.
